title: Document-Model Common Metadata Production Contract
summary: Format-neutral rules for common metadata storage across layer, prim, attribute, relationship, and variant specs. Both USDA and USDC mapping productions cite this contract for the field-vocabulary discipline and listOp subfield semantics shared across formats.

excluded_fields.policy: Strict AOUSD §7.6 conformance (no deprecated / out-of-scope / non-spec field support). The names below are recognized as reserved spec tokens and rejected (ForbiddenFieldOnSpecKind): they are NOT admitted as live fields by spec_kind_field_admission and must NOT be reinterpreted as generic user/extension fields. Tokens with no spec basis at all are removed outright. These were previously carried to mirror OpenUSD's SdfFieldKeys/SdfChildrenKeys; the live vocabulary above is now the AOUSD §7.6 live field set only.

removed_no_spec_basis.note: Absent from the AOUSD spec and not OpenUSD field keys either; removed outright (were previously listed as attribute fields).

spec_kind_field_admission.rule: Each spec kind admits only the union of (shared_across_all_spec_kinds + that-kind's-specific-fields). A field belonging to a different spec kind's specific set must be rejected, regardless of whether the source format encodes it. Format-specific mapping contracts pin the rejection mechanism (USDA: reserved keyword in metadata context; USDC: field-name token lookup on the matching spec form).

matrix.prim: shared + prim_only_composition_arcs + primChildren + propertyChildren + variantSetChildren + primOrder + propertyOrder + typeName + specifier
matrix.variant: shared + prim_only_composition_arcs + primChildren + propertyChildren + variantSetChildren + specifier

listop_subfields.authored_subfield_rule: Each listOp field stores up to one value per subfield. When the same subfield is authored multiple times within a single layer (e.g. via repeated prepend statements), last-one-wins per subfield. Cross-layer composition is out of scope.
listop_subfields.single_item_is_explicit: When a source format authors a single item where a listOp is allowed without an explicit listOp prefix, the result is an explicit listOp containing that one item.
listop_subfields.none_is_empty_subfield: When a source format authors None where a listOp is allowed, the result is an empty listOp on the named subfield.

repeated_field_semantics.non_listop_fields: Within a single source layer, the last authored value wins for a non-listOp metadata field with the same name.
repeated_field_semantics.listop_subfields: Within a single source layer, the last authored value wins per listOp subfield (not per listOp as a whole).

extension_field_rule.rule: Only spellings that are not known core or kind-specific field names may use document-model extension field-token lookup. Reserved spellings collide if used as extension fields; emitting a known field name through the extension path is a violation. Each format's mapping contract enumerates the reserved set (including any format-specific keyword aliases) for that format's parser dispatch.
extension_field_rule.interpretation: Unknown extension metadata fields are validated as field names and interned into the same field-name token identity domain as core fields, but their spelling must not collide with the known core vocabulary.

diagnostics.rule: All metadata-related diagnostics use stable codes that goldens can match. The canonical shape per contracts/diagnostics.schema.json is {severity, code, message, [path], [line], [column]}.
diagnostics.format_specific_codes_must_reduce: Format-specific diagnostics (InvalidUsdcHeader, UnsupportedUsdcVersion, MalformedUsdcSpecForm, etc., and the USDA equivalents) must reduce to a format-neutral code when surfaced through layer-open dispatch, so the document-model layer can match without format detection.

laws: metadata field-name identity is via token, not raw string keys
each spec kind admits only the union of shared fields plus its kind-specific fields; emitting a non-admitted field on a spec kind is a contract violation
listOp metadata uses the {explicit, prepend, append, delete, reorder} subfield set; subfields are stored independently
a single item authored where a listOp is allowed becomes an explicit listOp
None authored where a listOp is allowed becomes an empty listOp on the named subfield
within a single source layer, repeated non-listOp metadata fields use last-one-wins; repeated listOp subfields use last-one-wins per subfield
every metadata diagnostic must use a stable code that goldens can match exactly
format-specific diagnostic codes reduce to format-neutral codes when surfaced through layer-open dispatch
golden_groups: generic metadata (comment, documentation, displayName, customData) on each spec kind
layer-only metadata authored on layer spec
prim-only metadata rejected on attribute, relationship, variantSet, and variant specs
attribute-only metadata rejected on prim and relationship specs
relationship-only metadata rejected on prim and attribute specs
listOp metadata subfield replacement within a single layer
None listOp metadata producing an empty subfield
single-item listOp authoring producing an explicit subfield
